Fern P orrrrcer- PRACTTcEs C ouurssroN
428 J Street r Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 10,2007
Mr. Daniel Vanderpriem
730 Puerto Place
Hayward, Califomia 94541
RE
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-07-024
Dear Mr. Vanderpriem:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post_
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act-,,).1 please^note that our
advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the
application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as cofirmon law conflict of
interest or Government Code Section 1090 which prohibits public officials from having
an economic interest in their agencies' contracts. You may contact the State Attorney
General's office for questions about Government code section 1090.
QUESTTON
Having recently left your position as the City of Oakland's Redevelopment
Agency Director, are you prohibited by the post-employment provisions of tire political
Reform Act from accepting a contract for consulting services during your retirement from
a company that is obtaining a loan from the oakland Redevelopmeit Agency?
CONCLUSION
The Act's one-year ban on a local of{icial's making an appearance before or
communication with his or her former agency to influenc. t"girlutire or administrative
action (Section 87406.3) applies to local elected officials andto chief administrators of
cities, counties, and special dishicts. A redevelopment agency is not a special district and
is not included within the scope of Section 87406.3. Thus, th! Act,s on"-y"* ban for
local officials does not apply to you after leaving your position as Directoi of the
Oakland Redevelopment Agency.
' Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Commission Regulations appear attitle 2,Sections 1g109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
ry


File No. A-07-024
Page No. 2
FACTS
You officially left office March 2,2007. In Novemb er 2006prior to your
decision to retire, a company requested a pre-development loan frorrrthe redevelopment
agency, a colnmon occurrence. Your department made a positive recommendation on the
loan, and the Redevelopment Agency Board (City Councii) granted the loan, although the
loan agreement had not been executed as of Febru ffiy 7,2007, and was still being
negotiated. You left active employment with the City of Oakland on January lg:2007,
and are no longer going in to work. You were still technically an employeeLntil March
2,2007, because you were using your vacation reave before retiring. 
-
Since Decernber 2006, you have not had any involvement in the loan agreement
being drafted by the city Attorney's office. The company contacted you upon your
retirement January 19,2007, and invited you to submit a proposal to ihem ibr consulting
services when you were no longer an employee of the city olOakland or Redevelopmeit
Agency, i.e., after March 2,2007. None of the loan proceeds from the Redevelopment
Agency would be used to pay you for your services. The developer would pay for your
services out of their own funds.
ANALYSIS
Local governmental officials who leave governmental service are subject to the
Act's one-year ban for local officials in Secti on 87406.3.2 Generally, this reJtriction
prohibits certain former local officials from communicating,for compensation, with his
or her former agency for the purpose of influencing any legislative oi adminis irative
actions including quasiJegislative and quasi-judicial actions.
Section 87 a06.3 (a) provides :
"(a) A local elected official, chief administrative officer of a county,
city manager, or general manager or chief administrator of a special
district who held a position with a local government agency as de{ined in
section 8204f shall not, for a period of one year after"l"urirg that office
or employment, act as agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent, for
compensation, any other person, by making any formal or informal
appearance before, or by making any oral or written communication to,
2 Local officials leaving govemment service are also subject to restrictions when negotiating prospective
employment. Prior to separation from government service, a public official is prohibiied undii Section
87407 and Regulation 18747 frommaking, participating in making, or influencing a..governmental
decision directly relating to any person with whom he oi she is nelotiating, or halany"aoangLent
concerning, prospective employment." Because you have alreadyleft yourposition as Direc-tor of the City
of Oakland's Redevelopment Agency, it is not necessary to addriss the restrictions on negotiating
prospective employment.
' Unde. Section 82047',"Local government agency' means a county, city or district of any kind including
school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision, or'any department, division, bureau,
office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing.,,


FileNo. A-07-024
Page No. 3
that local govemment agency, or any committee, subcommittee, orpresent
member of that local government agency, or any officer or employee of
the local govemment agency, if the appearanceor communication is made
for the pulpose of influencing administrativea or legislatives action, or
influencing any action or proceeding involving the ir.,rur,"", amendment,
awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale
or purchase of goods or property.,,
The one-year ban of Section 87406.3 applies to certain anployees or officers of
local governmental agencies including local elected officials, chieiadministrative officers
of counties, city managers, and general managers or chief administrators of special
districts. (Section 87 a06.3 (a).)
In addition, Regulation 18746.3(a) states:
"(a) The prohibitions of Government code section g7406.3 apply to a
public official who, on or after July l, 2006,holds a position as a:
"(1) Local elected official;
"(2) Chief administrative officer of a county;
"(3) city manager, including the chief administrator of a city; or
"(4) Gener al manager or chief administrator of a special district who
holds a position with a local govemment agency as defined by
Government code section 82041, including the general manager or chief
administrator of an air pollution control district or air quality management
district."
You recently left the position as Director of the Oakland Redevelopment
Agency where you were an ernployee of the city of oakland. The oakland city
council sits as the Board of the oakland Redevelopment Agency.
As the director of the oakland Redevelopment Agency, you did not hold a
position as chief administrator of a city or county. The question to examine is
whether you are covered under the one-year ban for locai officials because you
were a general manager or chief administrator of aspecial district. (Section
87 406.3 (a) and Regulation I 87 46.3(a)(a).)
n Under Section 87406-3(dX1), "'[a]dminiskative action' means the proposal, drafting, development,
consideration, amendment, enactment, or defeat by any local govemment agency of any matter, including
any rule, regulation, or other action in any regulatoryproceeding, whether q:uasllte$sl;ti"" o, qruri-
judicial- Administrative action does not include any action that is solely ministeriaL"
t Undet Section 87406.3(d)(2), "'[]egislative action'means the drafting, introduction, modification,
enactment, defeat, approval, or veto of any ordinance, amendment, r"rolrrtiorr, report, oominatio.r, or other
matter by the legislative body of a local gove(unext agency or by any committee or subcommittee thereof,
o1-by a member or employee of the legislative body of the local gor"-*"rrt agency acting in his or her
oflicial capacity)'


File No. A-07-024
Page No. 4
A special district is defined in Government code Section 16271(d), in
part, as follows:
"(d) 'Special district' means any ageicy of the state for the
local perfornance of govemmental orproprietary functions within
limited boundaries. 'special district' includes a county service
area, amaintenance district or area, an improvanent district or
improvement zone, or any other zone or area formed for the
pu{pose of designating an area within which a property tax rate
will be levied to pay for a service or improvemenlbenefiting that
area...."
A special district is a form of local government that can raise revenues to
deliver specific public services within defined boundaries. Examples of special
districts include water districts, county sanitation districts, hospital districis, fire
protection dishicts, mosquito abatement diskicts, parks and recreation districts,
flood control districts, cemetery districts, etc.
You worked for the oakland Redevelopment Agency. According to the
Community Redeve.lopment Agencies Annual Report p"ttirl"a by the Clafifornia State
Controller's Office,o community redevelopment funds are used to assist local
govemments in reconstruction and rehabilitation of those areas in various communities
that suffer from unsafe, unfit, deteriorated, and economically dislocated buildings and
properties. Under the state Community Redevelopment Law, funds from local property
taxes are used to promote the redevelopment ofblighted areas.
However, a redevelopment agency is different from a special district. The
California Supreme Court has held that a redevelopmant agenry is not a..special district,,
within the meaning of Cal. Const., art. XIII A, $ 4, which requiies a twothirds vote of
the electorate for imposition of special taxes, because a redevelopment agenry does not
have the power to levy taxes. Huntington Park Redevelopment )gency i Mirtin,3g Cal-
3d 100 (1985). The Court observed that redevelopment agencies-are not empowered to,
and do not in fact, levy property taxes. Instead the county tax collector collects taxes
used by redevelopment agencies- Id. at 106.
The Special Districts Annual Report, published by the State Controller,s Office,
states that "[s]pecial districts are defined as legally constituted governmental entities that
are not cities, counties, redevelopment agencies, or school districts. ...,,7 (Emphasis
added.) In addition, in discussing special districts, a publication of the SenatJLocal
Government Committee states that "special districts are not redevelopment agencies.,,s
6 off'ce of the California State Controller, Community Redevelopment Agencies Annual Reportfor Fiscal
Year ended June 30, 2005,p. iii (21"ted. 2006).
' Offt"" of the California. State Controller, Special Districts Annual Reportfor Fiscql year ended June 30,
2004,p. iii (54n ed,.2006).


File No. A-07_024
Page No. 5
You retired as Director of the city of oakland Redevelopment Agency. Because
you were not a local elected official, a chief administrative officlr oru 
"iTy o. county, or
a chief administrator of a special district, the Act's or"-y"u, ban for local officials in
Section 87406.3 does not apply to you.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (gl6) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
X,tA f. d"fw*t
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
HPWjgl

8 Kimia Mizany & April Manatt, California State Senate Local Government Committee, What's So Special
About Special Districts? A Citizen's Guide to Special Districts in California, p. 3 (3'd ed. 2002.)

Agency Director, are you prohibited by the post-employment provisions of tire political
Reform Act from accepting a contract for consulting services during your retirement from
a company that is obtaining a loan from the oakland Redevelopmeit Agency?

CONCLUSION: The Act's one-year ban on a local of{icial's making an appearance before or
communication with his or her former agency to influenc. t"girlutire or administrative
action (Section 87406.3) applies to local elected officials andto chief administrators of
cities, counties, and special dishicts. A redevelopment agency is not a special district and
is not included within the scope of Section 87406.3. Thus, th! Act,s on"-y"* ban for
local officials does not apply to you after leaving your position as Directoi of the
Oakland Redevelopment Agency.
